justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from user-controlled HTTP input clearly noted, fix is parameterized queries (not sanitization), safe_to_deploy is false/BLOCK, confidence.level 99 >= 80.

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with clear reproduction steps
Fix specifies parameterized queries with concrete code example
safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict
SELECT * exposure noted as secondary finding at High severity (within acceptable range)
Confidence at 99 with well-reasoned high/low confidence areas
Bonus: identified authorization gap and missing pagination as important issues

agent_output_excerpt: {"summary":{"verdict":"BLOCK","one_liner":"SQL injection vulnerability in public-facing endpoint — direct string interpolation of user input into raw SQL."},"critical_findings":[{"title":"SQL Injection via unsanitized query parameters","risk":"Attacker can exfiltrate, modify, or destroy the entire database by injecting SQL through customer_id or status_filter.","severity":"Critical","location":"search_deliveries function, lines 2-6 (f-string query construction)","reproduction":"GET /deliveries?c
